# FSharp.Data.Fred

## Docs

### [F# Data FRED](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/index.md)

F# Data FRED
F# Data FRED is a library for
FRED database data access
based on FSharp.Data.
Short for Federal Reserve Economic Data, FRED is an online database consisting
of hundreds of thousands of economic data time series from scores of national,
international, public, and private sources.
This library uses the FRED API to access the data.
You can use FSharp.Data.Fred in dotnet interactive
notebooks in Visual Studio Code
or Jupyter, or in F# scripts (.fsx files),
by referencing the package as follows:
#r "nuget: FSharp.Data.Fred, 0.3.0"
#r "nuget: FSharp.Data" //Also load FSharp.Data

open FSharp.Data
open FSharp.Data.Fred
open System //Usefull to access DateTime

F# Data FRED
First in order to use the methods you'll need to create a value with type Fred.
Fred receives an API key as a parameter (string). Get your FRED API key.
Example:
let apiKey = "insert API key here"
Now you can use the created value myFred to access all the methods in the FRED library.
FRED.Series Module Functions:

Search.
Info.
Categories.
Release.
Tags.
Observations.

F# Data FRED: Series.Search
A collection of economic data series that match the parameters.
The parameters are the following:

searchText, required.
The words to match against economic data series.

searchType, optional. Default is SearchType.FullText.
Determines the type of search to perform.
One of the following: SearchType.FullText or SearchType.SeriesId.

SearchType.FullText searches series attributes title, units, frequency, and tags by parsing words into stems. 
This makes it possible for searches like 'Industry' to match series containing related words such as 'Industries'.
Of course, you can search for multiple words like 'money' and 'stock'. Remember to url encode spaces (e.g. 'money%20stock').

SearchType.SeriesId performs a substring search on series IDs. Searching for 'ex' will find series containing 'ex' anywhere in a series ID.
'' can be used to anchor searches and match 0 or more of any character. Searching for 'ex' will find series containing 'ex' at the beginning of a series ID.
Searching for 'ex' will find series containing 'ex' at the end of a series ID. It's also possible to put an '' in the middle of a string.
'm*sl' finds any series starting with 'm' and ending with 'sl'.

realtimeStart, optional. Default: today's date.
The start of the real-time period. For more information, see Real-Time Periods.

realtimeEnd, optional. Default: today's date.
The start of the real-time period. For more information, see Real-Time Periods.

limit, optional. Default: 1000.
The maximum number of results to return.
integer between 1 and 1000.

orderBy, optional. Default: If the value of searchType is SearchType.FullText then the default value of orderBy is SearchOrder.SearchRank. If the value of searchType is SearchType.SeriesId then the default value of orderBy is SearchOrder.SeriesId.
Order results by values of the specified attribute.
One of the following:
SearchOrder.SearchRank SearchOrder.SeriesIdOrder SearchOrder.Title SearchOrder.Units SearchOrder.Frequency SearchOrder.SeasonalAdjustment
SearchOrder.RealTimeStart SearchOrder.RealTimeEnd SearchOrder.LastUpdated SearchOrder.ObservationStart SearchOrder.ObservationEnd SearchOrder.Popularity SearchOrder.GroupPopularity

sortOrder, optional. Default: If orderBy is equal to SearchOrder.SearchRank or SearchOrder.Popularity, then the default value of sortOrder is SortOrder.Descending. Otherwise, the default sortOrder is SortOrder.Ascending.
Sort results is ascending or descending order for attribute values specified by orderBy.

Examples:
Search for "10-Year" text without specifying any optional parameters.
myFred.Series.Search("10-Year").Summary()

Count of search hits: 10216
Top 10 results:
  1. Market Yield on U.S. Treasury Securities at 10-Year Constant Maturity, Quoted on an Investment Basis 
         Id: DGS10      Period: 1962-01-02 to 2026-04-07  Freq: Daily 

  2. 10-Year Treasury Constant Maturity Minus 2-Year Treasury Constant Maturity 
         Id: T10Y2Y     Period: 1976-06-01 to 2026-04-08  Freq: Daily 

  3. Market Yield on U.S. Treasury Securities at 10-Year Constant Maturity, Quoted on an Investment Basis 
         Id: GS10       Period: 1953-04-01 to 2026-03-01  Freq: Monthly 

  4. 10-Year Treasury Constant Maturity Minus 2-Year Treasury Constant Maturity 
         Id: T10Y2YM    Period: 1976-06-01 to 2026-03-01  Freq: Monthly 

  5. Market Yield on U.S. Treasury Securities at 10-Year Constant Maturity, Quoted on an Investment Basis 
         Id: WGS10YR    Period: 1962-01-05 to 2026-04-03  Freq: Weekly, Ending Friday 

  6. Market Yield on U.S. Treasury Securities at 10-Year Constant Maturity, Quoted on an Investment Basis 
         Id: RIFLGFCY10NA Period: 1962-01-01 to 2025-01-01  Freq: Annual 

  7. 10-Year Treasury Constant Maturity Minus 3-Month Treasury Constant Maturity 
         Id: T10Y3M     Period: 1982-01-04 to 2026-04-08  Freq: Daily 

  8. 10-Year Treasury Constant Maturity Minus 3-Month Treasury Constant Maturity 
         Id: T10Y3MM    Period: 1982-01-01 to 2026-03-01  Freq: Monthly 

  9. 10-Year Breakeven Inflation Rate 
         Id: T10YIE     Period: 2003-01-02 to 2026-04-08  Freq: Daily 

 10. Market Yield on U.S. Treasury Securities at 10-Year Constant Maturity, Quoted on an Investment Basis, Inflation-Indexed 
         Id: DFII10     Period: 2003-01-02 to 2026-04-07  Freq: Daily
Search for "10-Year" text specifying some optional parameters.
myFred.Series.Search("10-Year", 
                     searchType = SearchType.FullText, 
                     limit = 3, 
                     orderBy = SearchOrder.GroupPopularity, 
                     sortOrder = SortOrder.Descending).Summary()

Count of search hits: 10216
Top 3 results:
  1. Market Yield on U.S. Treasury Securities at 10-Year Constant Maturity, Quoted on an Investment Basis 
         Id: DGS10      Period: 1962-01-02 to 2026-04-07  Freq: Daily 

  2. Market Yield on U.S. Treasury Securities at 10-Year Constant Maturity, Quoted on an Investment Basis 
         Id: GS10       Period: 1953-04-01 to 2026-03-01  Freq: Monthly 

  3. Market Yield on U.S. Treasury Securities at 10-Year Constant Maturity, Quoted on an Investment Basis 
         Id: WGS10YR    Period: 1962-01-05 to 2026-04-03  Freq: Weekly, Ending Friday
Search for "Hello World" text that should not match any series.
myFred.Series.Search("Hello World").Summary()

Count of search hits: 0
Top 0 results:
F# Data FRED: Series.Info
Get the information for an economic data series.
This method only asks for the series id as a parameter (string)
Examples:
Get the information for series id = "GS10"
myFred.Series.Info "GS10"

val it: JsonProvider<...>.InfoResponse =
  {
  "realtime_start": "2026-04-09",
  "realtime_end": "2026-04-09",
  "seriess": [
    {
      "id": "GS10",
      "realtime_start": "2026-04-09",
      "realtime_end": "2026-04-09",
      "title": "Market Yield on U.S. Treasury Securities at 10-Year Constant Maturity, Quoted on an Investment Basis",
      "observation_start": "1953-04-01",
      "observation_end": "2026-03-01",
      "frequency": "Monthly",
      "frequency_short": "M",
      "units": "Percent",
      "units_short": "%",
      "seasonal...
F# Data FRED: Series.Categories
Get the categories for an economic data series.
The parameters are the following:

id, required.
The id for a series (string).

realtimeStart, optional. Default: today's date.
The start of the real-time period. For more information, see Real-Time Periods.

realtimeEnd, optional. Default: today's date.
The start of the real-time period. For more information, see Real-Time Periods.

Examples:
Get the category information for series id = "GS10"
myFred.Series.Categories "GS10"

val it: JsonProvider<...>.CategoriesResponse =
  {
  "categories": [
    {
      "id": 115,
      "name": "Treasury Constant Maturity",
      "parent_id": 22
    }
  ]
}
You can also access the category fields with Array.map:
myFred.Series.Categories("GS10").Categories
|> Array.map(fun x -> x.Name)

val it: string array = [|"Treasury Constant Maturity"|]
F# Data FRED: Series.Release
Get the release for an economic data series.
The parameters are the following:

id, required.
The id for a series (string).

realtimeStart, optional. Default: today's date.
The start of the real-time period. For more information, see Real-Time Periods.

realtimeEnd, optional. Default: today's date.
The start of the real-time period. For more information, see Real-Time Periods.

Examples:
Get the release information for series id = "GS10"
myFred.Series.Release "GS10"

val it: JsonProvider<...>.ReleaseResponse =
  {
  "realtime_start": "2026-04-09",
  "realtime_end": "2026-04-09",
  "releases": [
    {
      "id": 18,
      "realtime_start": "2026-04-09",
      "realtime_end": "2026-04-09",
      "name": "H.15 Selected Interest Rates",
      "press_release": true,
      "link": "http://www.federalreserve.gov/releases/h15/",
      "notes": "For questions on the data, please contact the data source: https://www.federalreserve.gov/apps/ContactUs/feedback.aspx?refurl=/releases/h15/%\r\nFor questions on FRED functional...
F# Data FRED: Series.Tags
Get the FRED tags for a series.
The parameters are the following:

id, required.
The id for a series (string).

realtimeStart, optional. Default: today's date.
The start of the real-time period. For more information, see Real-Time Periods.

realtimeEnd, optional. Default: today's date.
The start of the real-time period. For more information, see Real-Time Periods.

orderBy, optional. Default: OrderByTags.SeriesCount.
Order results by values of the specified attribute.
One of the following:
OrderByTags.SeriesCount, OrderByTags.Popularity, OrderByTags.Created, OrderByTags.Name, OrderByTags.GroupId.

sortOrder, optional. Default: SortOrder.Ascending.
Sort results is ascending or descending order for attribute values specified by orderBy.

Examples:
Get the first 3 tags information for series id = "GS10" without specifying any optional parameters.
myFred.Series.Tags("GS10").Tags
|> Array.truncate 3 

val it: JsonProvider<...>.Tag array =
  [|{
  "name": "h15",
  "group_id": "rls",
  "notes": "H.15 Selected Interest Rates",
  "created": "2012-08-16 15:21:17-05",
  "popularity": 54,
  "series_count": 276
};
    {
  "name": "10-year",
  "group_id": "gen",
  "notes": "",
  "created": "2012-02-27 10:18:19-06",
  "popularity": 52,
  "series_count": 290
};
    {
  "name": "interest rate",
  "group_id": "gen",
  "notes": "",
  "created": "2012-05-29 10:14:19-05",
  "popularity": 68,
  "series_count": 1642
}|]
Get the first 3 tags information for series id = "GS10" specifying some optional parameters.
myFred.Series.Tags("GS10", orderBy = OrderByTags.Popularity, sortOrder = SortOrder.Descending).Tags
|> Array.truncate 3 

val it: JsonProvider<...>.Tag array =
  [|{
  "name": "h15",
  "group_id": "rls",
  "notes": "H.15 Selected Interest Rates",
  "created": "2012-08-16 15:21:17-05",
  "popularity": 54,
  "series_count": 272
};
    {
  "name": "10-year",
  "group_id": "gen",
  "notes": "",
  "created": "2012-02-27 10:18:19-06",
  "popularity": 52,
  "series_count": 300
};
    {
  "name": "interest rate",
  "group_id": "gen",
  "notes": "",
  "created": "2012-05-29 10:14:19-05",
  "popularity": 68,
  "series_count": 1616
}|]
F# Data FRED: Series.Observations
Get the observations or data values for an economic data series.
The parameters are the following:

id, required.
The id for a series (string).

realtimeStart, optional. Default: today's date.
The start of the real-time period. For more information, see Real-Time Periods.

realtimeEnd, optional. Default: today's date.
The start of the real-time period. For more information, see Real-Time Periods.

limit, optional. Default: 1000.
The maximum number of results to return.
integer between 1 and 1000.

sortOrder, optional. Default: SortOrder.Ascending.
Sort results is ascending or descending observation date order.

observationStart, optional. Default: DateTime(1776,07,04) (earliest available).
The start of the observation period. DateTime(yyyy,MM,dd) formatted DateTime.

observationEnd, optional. Default: today's date.   DateTime(9999,12,31) (latest available).
The end of the observation period. DateTime(yyyy,MM,dd) formatted DateTime.

units, optional. Default: Units.Levels (No transformation).
A key that indicates a data value transformation.
One of the following:
Units.Levels
Units.Change
Units.ChangefromYearAgo
Units.PercentChange
Units.PercentChangefromYearAgo
Units.CompoundedAnnualRateofChange
Units.ContinuouslyCompoundedRateofChange
Units.ContinuouslyCompoundedAnnualRateofChange
Units.NaturalLog.
Unit transformation formulas

frequency, optional.
An optional parameter that indicates a lower frequency to aggregate values to.
The FRED frequency aggregation feature converts higher frequency data series into lower frequency
data series (e.g. converts a monthly data series into an annual data series).
In FRED, the highest frequency data is daily, and the lowest frequency data is annual.
There are 3 aggregation methods available- average, sum, and end of period.
See the aggregation_method parameter.
One of the following:
Frequency.Daily, Frequency.Weekly, Frequency.Biweekly, Frequency.Monthly, Frequency.Quarterly,
Frequency.Semiannual, Frequency.Annual, Frequency.WeeklyEndingFriday,
Frequency.WeeklyEndingThursday, Frequency.WeeklyEndingWednesday, Frequency.WeeklyEndingTuesday,
Frequency.WeeklyEndingMonday, Frequency.WeeklyEndingSunday, Frequency.WeeklyEndingSaturday,
Frequency.BiweeklyEndingWednesday, Frequency.BiweeklyEndingMonday.

aggMethod, optional. Default: AggMethod.Average.
A key that indicates the aggregation method used for frequency aggregation.
This parameter has no affect if the frequency parameter is not set.
One of the following:
AggMethod.Average, AggMethod.Sum, AggMethod.EndOfPeriod.

Examples:
Get the observations for "GS10" series without specifying any optional parameters.
myFred.Series.Observations("GS10").Observations
|> Seq.truncate 3 

val it: JsonProvider<...>.Observation seq =
  seq
    [{
  "realtime_start": "2026-04-09",
  "realtime_end": "2026-04-09",
  "date": "1953-04-01",
  "value": "2.83"
};
     {
  "realtime_start": "2026-04-09",
  "realtime_end": "2026-04-09",
  "date": "1953-05-01",
  "value": "3.05"
};
     {
  "realtime_start": "2026-04-09",
  "realtime_end": "2026-04-09",
  "date": "1953-06-01",
  "value": "3.11"
}]
Get the observations for "DTP10J25" with a semi-annual frequency.
myFred.Series.Observations("DTP10J25", frequency = Frequency.Semiannual).Observations
|> Seq.truncate 3 

val it: JsonProvider<...>.Observation seq =
  seq
    [{
  "realtime_start": "2026-04-09",
  "realtime_end": "2026-04-09",
  "date": "2015-01-01",
  "value": "."
};
     {
  "realtime_start": "2026-04-09",
  "realtime_end": "2026-04-09",
  "date": "2015-07-01",
  "value": "0.624"
};
     {
  "realtime_start": "2026-04-09",
  "realtime_end": "2026-04-09",
  "date": "2016-01-01",
  "value": "0.315"
}]

## API Reference

### [FSharp.Data.Fred](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/reference/fsharp-data-fred.html)

QueryParameters 
Fred 
Search 
Series

### [QueryParameters](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/reference/fsharp-data-fred-queryparameters.html)

QueryParameters 
 Contains types for specifying search query parameters.  
QueryParameters.AggMethod 
AggMethod 
QueryParameters.Frequency 
Frequency 
QueryParameters.OrderByTags 
OrderByTags 
QueryParameters.SearchOrder 
SearchOrder 
QueryParameters.SearchType 
SearchType 
QueryParameters.SortOrder 
SortOrder 
QueryParameters.Units 
Units

### [AggMethod](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/reference/fsharp-data-fred-queryparameters-aggmethod.html)

AggMethod 

   A key that indicates the aggregation method used for frequency aggregation. 
   This parameter has no affect if the frequency parameter is not set.   
    
AggMethod.IsSum 
IsSum 
AggMethod.IsEndOfPeriod 
IsEndOfPeriod 
AggMethod.IsAverage 
IsAverage 
AggMethod.Average 
Average 
AggMethod.Sum 
Sum 
AggMethod.EndOfPeriod 
EndOfPeriod

### [AggMethod.IsSum](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/reference/fsharp-data-fred-queryparameters-aggmethod.html#IsSum)

AggMethod.IsSum 
IsSum

### [AggMethod.IsEndOfPeriod](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/reference/fsharp-data-fred-queryparameters-aggmethod.html#IsEndOfPeriod)

AggMethod.IsEndOfPeriod 
IsEndOfPeriod

### [AggMethod.IsAverage](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/reference/fsharp-data-fred-queryparameters-aggmethod.html#IsAverage)

AggMethod.IsAverage 
IsAverage

### [AggMethod.Average](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/reference/fsharp-data-fred-queryparameters-aggmethod.html#Average)

AggMethod.Average 
Average 
 Computes the average to get the observation values for the desired frequency.

### [AggMethod.Sum](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/reference/fsharp-data-fred-queryparameters-aggmethod.html#Sum)

AggMethod.Sum 
Sum 
 Computes the sum to get the observation values for the desired frequency.

### [AggMethod.EndOfPeriod](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/reference/fsharp-data-fred-queryparameters-aggmethod.html#EndOfPeriod)

AggMethod.EndOfPeriod 
EndOfPeriod 
 Set the end of period value to get the observation values for the desired frequency.

### [Frequency](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/reference/fsharp-data-fred-queryparameters-frequency.html)

Frequency 

   The FRED frequency aggregation feature converts higher frequency data series into lower frequency 
   data series (e.g. converts a monthly data series into an annual data series). 
   In FRED, the highest frequency data is daily, and the lowest frequency data is annual.  
  
Frequency.IsDefault 
IsDefault 
Frequency.IsWeeklyEndingThursday 
IsWeeklyEndingThursday 
Frequency.IsBiweeklyEndingWednesday 
IsBiweeklyEndingWednesday 
Frequency.IsBiweeklyEndingMonday 
IsBiweeklyEndingMonday 
Frequency.IsWeeklyEndingWednesday 
IsWeeklyEndingWednesday 
Frequency.IsWeeklyEndingMonday 
IsWeeklyEndingMonday 
Frequency.IsAnnual 
IsAnnual 
Frequency.IsWeekly 
IsWeekly 
Frequency.IsDaily 
IsDaily 
Frequency.IsWeeklyEndingFriday 
IsWeeklyEndingFriday 
Frequency.IsWeeklyEndingSaturday 
IsWeeklyEndingSaturday 
Frequency.IsWeeklyEndingSunday 
IsWeeklyEndingSunday 
Frequency.IsSemiannual 
IsSemiannual 
Frequency.IsBiweekly 
IsBiweekly 
Frequency.IsWeeklyEndingTuesday 
IsWeeklyEndingTuesday 
Frequency.IsMonthly 
IsMonthly 
Frequency.IsQuarterly 
IsQuarterly 
Frequency.Daily 
Daily 
Frequency.Weekly 
Weekly 
Frequency.Biweekly 
Biweekly 
Frequency.Monthly 
Monthly 
Frequency.Quarterly 
Quarterly 
Frequency.Semiannual 
Semiannual 
Frequency.Annual 
Annual 
Frequency.WeeklyEndingFriday 
WeeklyEndingFriday 
Frequency.WeeklyEndingThursday 
WeeklyEndingThursday 
Frequency.WeeklyEndingWednesday 
WeeklyEndingWednesday 
Frequency.WeeklyEndingTuesday 
WeeklyEndingTuesday 
Frequency.WeeklyEndingMonday 
WeeklyEndingMonday 
Frequency.WeeklyEndingSunday 
WeeklyEndingSunday 
Frequency.WeeklyEndingSaturday 
WeeklyEndingSaturday 
Frequency.BiweeklyEndingWednesday 
BiweeklyEndingWednesday 
Frequency.BiweeklyEndingMonday 
BiweeklyEndingMonday 
Frequency.Default 
Default

### [Frequency.WeeklyEndingMonday](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/reference/fsharp-data-fred-queryparameters-frequency.html#WeeklyEndingMonday)

Frequency.WeeklyEndingMonday

### [SearchType](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/reference/fsharp-data-fred-queryparameters-searchtype.html)

SearchType 

   Represents the type of search to perform.
    
SearchType.IsFullText 
IsFullText 
SearchType.IsSeriesId 
IsSeriesId 
SearchType.FullText 
FullText 
SearchType.SeriesId 
SeriesId

### [SearchType.IsFullText](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/reference/fsharp-data-fred-queryparameters-searchtype.html#IsFullText)

SearchType.IsFullText 
IsFullText

### [SearchType.IsSeriesId](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/reference/fsharp-data-fred-queryparameters-searchtype.html#IsSeriesId)

SearchType.IsSeriesId 
IsSeriesId

### [SearchType.FullText](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/reference/fsharp-data-fred-queryparameters-searchtype.html#FullText)

SearchType.FullText 
FullText 

 Searches series attributes title, units, frequency, and tags by parsing words into stems.
 This makes it possible for searches like 'Industry' to match series containing related words such as 'Industries'. 
 Of course, you can search for multiple words like 'money' and 'stock'. Remember to url encode spaces (e.g. <code>money%20stock</code>).

### [SearchType.SeriesId](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/reference/fsharp-data-fred-queryparameters-searchtype.html#SeriesId)

SearchType.SeriesId 
SeriesId 
 Performs a substring search on series IDs. Searching for 'ex' will find series containing 'ex' anywhere in a series ID.
 '*' can be used to anchor searches and match 0 or more of any character. Searching for 'ex*' will find series containing 'ex' at the beginning of a series ID.
 Searching for '*ex' will find series containing 'ex' at the end of a series ID. It's also possible to put an '*' in the middle of a string.
 'm*sl' finds any series starting with 'm' and ending with 'sl'.

### [SortOrder](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/reference/fsharp-data-fred-queryparameters-sortorder.html)

SortOrder 

    Sort results is ascending or descending observation date order.
    
SortOrder.IsDescending 
IsDescending 
SortOrder.IsAscending 
IsAscending 
SortOrder.Ascending 
Ascending 
SortOrder.Descending 
Descending

SortOrder.IsDescending 
IsDescending

### [SortOrder.IsAscending](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/reference/fsharp-data-fred-queryparameters-sortorder.html#IsAscending)

SortOrder.IsAscending 
IsAscending

SortOrder.Ascending 
Ascending 
 Sort results in ascending order.

### [SortOrder.Descending](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/reference/fsharp-data-fred-queryparameters-sortorder.html#Descending)

SortOrder.Descending 
Descending

### [Units.IsPercentChangeFromYearAgo](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/reference/fsharp-data-fred-queryparameters-units.html#IsPercentChangeFromYearAgo)

Units.IsPercentChangeFromYearAgo

Units.IsChange

Units.IsLevels

Units.IsPercentChange 
IsPercentChange

### [Units.IsContinuouslyCompoundedRateofChange](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/reference/fsharp-data-fred-queryparameters-units.html#IsContinuouslyCompoundedRateofChange)

Units.IsContinuouslyCompoundedRateofChange 
IsContinuouslyCompoundedRateofChange

### [Units.Levels](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/reference/fsharp-data-fred-queryparameters-units.html#Levels)

Units.Levels 
Levels 
 Observation(t), default.

### [Units.Change](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/reference/fsharp-data-fred-queryparameters-units.html#Change)

Units.Change 
Change 
 Observation(t) - Observation(t-1)

### [Units.ChangeFromYearAgo](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/reference/fsharp-data-fred-queryparameters-units.html#ChangeFromYearAgo)

Units.ChangeFromYearAgo 
ChangeFromYearAgo 
 Observation(t) - Observation(t-#ObsPerYear)

### [Units.PercentChange](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/reference/fsharp-data-fred-queryparameters-units.html#PercentChange)

Units.PercentChange 
PercentChange 
 ((Observation(t)/Observation(t-1)) - 1) * 100

### [Units.PercentChangeFromYearAgo](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/reference/fsharp-data-fred-queryparameters-units.html#PercentChangeFromYearAgo)

Units.PercentChangeFromYearAgo 
PercentChangeFromYearAgo 
 ((Observation(t)/Observation(t-#ObsPerYear)) - 1) * 100

### [Units.CompoundedAnnualRateofChange](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/reference/fsharp-data-fred-queryparameters-units.html#CompoundedAnnualRateofChange)

Units.CompoundedAnnualRateofChange 
CompoundedAnnualRateofChange 
 (((Observation(t)/Observation(t-1)) ** (#ObsPerYear)) - 1) * 100

### [Units.ContinuouslyCompoundedRateofChange](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/reference/fsharp-data-fred-queryparameters-units.html#ContinuouslyCompoundedRateofChange)

Units.ContinuouslyCompoundedRateofChange 
ContinuouslyCompoundedRateofChange 
 (ln(Observation(t)) - ln(Observation(t-1))) * 100

### [Units.ContinuouslyCompoundedAnnualRateofChange](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/reference/fsharp-data-fred-queryparameters-units.html#ContinuouslyCompoundedAnnualRateofChange)

Units.ContinuouslyCompoundedAnnualRateofChange 
ContinuouslyCompoundedAnnualRateofChange 
 ((ln(Observation(t)) - ln(Observation(t-1))) * 100) * #ObsPerYear

### [Units.NaturalLog](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/reference/fsharp-data-fred-queryparameters-units.html#NaturalLog)

Units.NaturalLog 
NaturalLog 
 ln(Observation(t))

### [Fred](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/reference/fsharp-data-fred-fred.html)

Fred 

   Represents a type for accessing the Fred API.
   You must provide an api key.
   
Fred.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor`` 
Fred.Key 
Key 
Fred.Series 
Series 
Fred.loadKey 
loadKey

Fred.Series 
Series 
 Represents a Fred data series.

Fred.loadKey 
loadKey 
 Loads a Fred api key from a json file.

Release

Series.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor``

### [Series.Categories](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/reference/fsharp-data-fred-series.html#Categories)

Series.Categories 
Categories 
 Get the categories for an economic data series.

### [Series.Info](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/reference/fsharp-data-fred-series.html#Info)

Series.Info 
Info 
 Get the information for an economic data series.

### [Series.Observations](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/reference/fsharp-data-fred-series.html#Observations)

Series.Observations 
Observations 
 Get the observations or data values for an economic data series.

### [Series.Release](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/reference/fsharp-data-fred-series.html#Release)

Series.Release 
Release 
 Get the release for an economic data series.

### [Series.Search](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/reference/fsharp-data-fred-series.html#Search)

Series.Search 
Search 
 Get economic data series that match search text.

### [Series.Tags](https://nhirschey.github.io/FSharp.Data.Fred/reference/fsharp-data-fred-series.html#Tags)

Series.Tags 
Tags 
 Get the FRED tags for a series.
